content: Do NOT include `Co-Authored-By: Claude ...` trailers in commit messages on this repo. The project convention is to keep commit history attribution-free (verified 2026-04-24: origin/main was rewritten to strip all such trailers; subsequent history has been kept clean). Claude Code's default commit template adds this trailer automatically — override it on every commit. If you notice trailers leaking in during autonomous work, strip them before push (`git filter-branch --msg-filter 'grep -v "^Co-Authored-By: Claude" || true' <range>`), not after.</content>
context: Discovered 2026-04-24 during the post-auto-session publish prep. Local clone had 29 unpushed commits (T-022/T-023/T-024/ISS-002 work + .story artifacts + version bump); `git push` was rejected because origin/main had been rewritten. Root-cause investigation found identical trees but different hashes on 22 parallel-path commits going back to the common ancestor `8fcbf6c`. Byte-diff of the first-diverged commit (`f4228e19` local vs `aac12926` remote) showed one difference: the remote version had `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>` stripped from the message. That cascade-rewrote every downstream commit's hash. User confirmed the rewrite was intentional (Option C: force-push local then run the same strip locally), so this is the project's durable convention, not a one-off cleanup. `git filter-branch --msg-filter 'grep -v "^Co-Authored-By: Claude" || true' main` rewrote 33 commits, tests still green (575/575), force-pushed cleanly to `d47e3c4`. Backup branch `backup/pre-strip-coauthor` kept locally. Future autonomous sessions must configure their commit template to skip the Claude trailer, or this history-rewrite dance happens every publish.
title: This project strips Co-Authored-By: Claude trailers from commit history
